description: User can open a browser dashboard that auto-detects the viewer timezone and shows a live local Claude promotion clock.
verify: Run the app
Open the dashboard in a browser
Confirm the timezone label matches the browser timezone
Confirm the live clock and countdown update without a refresh

description: User can see an infographic of historical peak and off-peak windows converted into the viewer timezone.
verify: Open the dashboard
Inspect the weekly matrix and history timeline
Confirm each window is labeled as peak or off-peak and shown in local time

description: User can see a forecasted next promotion campaign plus next likely off-peak and peak transitions computed from official published campaign history rather than hard-coded.
verify: Open the dashboard
Inspect the forecast card
Confirm the estimate includes next off-peak and next peak timestamps, confidence scores, and supporting rationale

description: The backend can discover official Claude promotion articles from the web and normalize them into campaign and window records without relying on local files.
verify: Start the API
Request /api/promotions
Confirm the response includes official source URLs, campaign records, and expanded windows

description: The React app follows the Figma-derived reference implementation while treating refs/design as a non-runtime reference bundle.
verify: Compare src/app and src/styles against refs/design
Confirm the runtime app does not import from refs/design

description: User can distinguish official live promotion status from historical or inferred pressure patterns, so the clock does not overstate certainty when no promotion is live.
verify: Open the dashboard outside an official promotion window
Confirm the clock says no promotion is live
Confirm the historical pattern is shown separately as an inference aid

description: Backend continues serving campaign and phase forecasts if holiday context data is unavailable.
verify: Simulate a holiday API failure
Request the promotion snapshot
Confirm the response still includes campaign, next off-peak, and next peak forecasts

description: Developer can generate repeatable Playwright audit screenshots and a runtime log for the live app.
verify: Start the app locally
Run npm run audit:screenshots
Confirm desktop and mobile screenshots plus a JSON audit log are produced

description: GitHub validates the app build and backend promotions API automatically on push and pull request.
verify: Push the repository to GitHub
Inspect the CI workflow run
Confirm it installs dependencies, runs npm run check, and smoke-tests /api/promotions

description: A Netlify production deploy serves the frontend and the promotions backend from the same domain so /api/promotions does not 404 in production.
verify: Deploy the current repository to Netlify
Request /api/promotions from the Netlify domain
Confirm the dashboard loads live promotion data instead of showing a 404 API error

description: Near-term peak and off-peak forecasts are based on the next few days of hourly historical pattern probabilities, not delayed until the next estimated public promotion campaign.
verify: Request the promotion snapshot outside an active official campaign
Confirm nextOffPeak and nextPeak fall within the next few hours or days when the historical pattern supports it
Confirm the campaign forecast remains separate as a long-range public promotion estimate

description: The chart and heatmap use real official window data only and display a rolling 7-day range ending today instead of a fixed Monday-to-Sunday aggregate.
verify: Build the dashboard model for a current date outside an active promotion
Confirm the last heatmap row is today and earlier rows step backward by calendar day
Confirm hours without official window coverage are marked as no-data rather than shown as inferred off-peak values

description: The production UI is consistently left-aligned LTR, avoids the old full-screen loading page, removes external Claude Help Center links, and shows the next future peak start instead of the current peak time.
verify: Load the app before data arrives and confirm the shell stays visible instead of a separate loading screen
Inspect the clock, forecast, history, and footer and confirm text is left-aligned LTR with no outbound help-page links
Confirm the next peak card points to a future peak start when the current local time is already inside a peak-pattern block

description: The clock explicitly states whether the app considers the current hour off-peak or not, using official live windows when available and the current inferred phase otherwise.
verify: Open the clock panel
Confirm it shows an 'Off-Peak Now' yes/no answer
Confirm the detail explains whether the answer comes from an official live window or from the current historical pattern

description: When no Help Center campaign is live, the app still anchors the current phase and near-term phase forecast to Anthropic's permanent 5 AM-11 AM PT weekday peak baseline, while keeping long-range campaign confidence conservative and the snapshot refresh cadence short.
verify: Request the promotion snapshot outside an active official campaign
Confirm nextOffPeak and nextPeak are still produced from the baseline-plus-history model
Build the dashboard model and confirm 'Off-Peak Now' flips from the permanent weekday baseline in the browser without waiting for a stale 10-minute cache
Confirm the long-range campaign confidence is reduced by the sample-size penalty when only one interval is available

description: The backend snapshot and UI surface live Claude status incidents, manual operator notes, and parser warnings so users can see operational context beyond official promotion windows.
verify: Request the promotion snapshot
Confirm the response includes statusPage, manualOverride, and parseWarnings fields
Load the dashboard and confirm the clock shows platform status and the notice strip can render manual overrides or parser warnings
